etymology.description: 有(有) (yǒu, "have; there is") and 时(時) (shí, "time") combine to describe an occurrence that happens occasionally by having certain times or instances.
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 有(有) (yǒu, "have; there is") and 时(時) (shí, "time") conceptualizes the idea of having certain times, which translates to the occasional or non-regular occurrence of something.

examples.english: Sometimes the weather is very hot.

examples.chinese: 他有时(他有時)会在早上跑步(會在早上跑步)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒushí huì zài zǎoshang pǎobù.
examples.english: He sometimes goes running in the morning.

examples.chinese: 我有时(我有時)在周末去看电影(在周末去看電影)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yǒushí zài zhōumò qù kàn diànyǐng.
examples.english: I sometimes go to watch movies on weekends.
